summary: Iteration 5 — Regression + new-feature verification for the 'Stop button on Real User Traffic' bug fix and the 4 newly-added pieces (forgiving stop endpoint, startup orphan reaper, GET /admin/system-check, GET .../jobs/{id}/live-log, _device_name_from_ua helper + device_name propagation). Regression (test_iteration2_smoke.py + test_rut_iteration4.py) = 52 passed / 1 skipped / 0 failed. New-feature suite (test_iteration5_new_features.py) = 33 passed / 0 failed. Combined = 85 pass / 1 skip / 0 fail. User-reported bug ('Stop button doesn't stop, no results shown / Job not running in this worker') is FIXED — stop endpoint now returns 200 in all 3 paths (in-memory running, DB-only terminal, DB-only running/orphan) and the startup reaper auto-stops leftover running/queued jobs on every backend boot.

minor.issue: 'Email service' check is reported as ok:false when neither SMTP_USER/SMTP_PASSWORD nor RESEND_API_KEY is set. In this environment none is configured, so overall status comes back 'degraded' (not 'healthy'). That is expected behaviour for this env, but in a production deploy this will always flip the overall badge red until SMTP is wired up. Consider treating 'no email service configured' as a warning rather than a hard failure, or flag it as info so that 'overall' stays 'healthy' when every other check is green.

'JWT secret' and 'Postback token' checks still fail when the defaults are kept. That is correct security hygiene, but it also means every fresh env will show overall=degraded even though nothing is actually broken. Document the expected state for a clean bring-up.

RUT background runner still does not cancel on uvicorn shutdown. With the new startup reaper the user-visible symptom is now mitigated (stale jobs get auto-stopped on next boot), but the worker can still hang on 'Waiting for background tasks to complete' during a live job restart.
find_click_across_dbs() still scans every per-user DB on every postback/pixel. Index clickid -> user_id in main_db to flatten O(N_users).

action_items: (Minor) Soften /api/admin/system-check so that missing Email / default JWT / default postback token flip 'overall' to 'degraded' only when at least one hard dependency (Python deps / Browser / Database / Storage) is red. Otherwise production admins will always see a degraded badge even on a perfectly healthy deploy.
(Carry-over) Cancel in-flight RUT background tasks on uvicorn shutdown — signals already exist (request_job_cancel); hook it from a FastAPI lifespan 'on_shutdown' so the worker never hangs on reload.
(Carry-over) Add a clickid -> user_id index in main_db so postback/pixel can stop scanning every per-user DB.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~9770 lines — consider splitting into routers (auth, admin, links, clicks, conversions, proxies, sub_users, real_user_traffic, form_filler, utilities). The RUT block alone is ~400 lines. The new /admin/system-check adds another ~140 lines.

_package_partial_results() silently swallows any zipfile error and returns None. Acceptable for best-effort, but the caller should surface a warning in the DB doc so admins know the stop succeeded but the zip did not.

The startup orphan reaper runs once in the @startup handler with no bounded concurrency — if the DB has 500+ orphan jobs the loop will serially call _package_partial_results 500x and block startup. Recommend asyncio.gather with a Semaphore(10) OR mark all jobs stopped first, then package in a background task.

GET /admin/system-check performs a shutil.disk_usage and importlib.import_module on every request. Cache the heavy bits for a few seconds or run under a TTL — otherwise a scripted status page call can become expensive.
_device_name_from_ua() regex chain is 30+ lines of conditionals; move the UA→device mapping table into a small YAML/JSON data file under /app/backend so UA updates do not require a code change.

success_rate.backend: 100% — regression 52 pass / 1 skip / 0 fail + new-features 33 pass / 0 fail = 85/1skip/0fail

test_credentials: Admin: admin@trackmaster.local / admin123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Fresh test_rut2_<uuid>@example.com created per run with real_user_traffic=true + all features + max_* quotas, cleaned via admin DELETE at the end.
seed_data_creation: Per-run (test_iteration5_new_features.py): 1 pending user -> activated with full feature set; 1 link (offer_url=https://example.com/rut-it5); 1 real RUT job for in-memory stop + live-log contract (proxies=1, UAs=1, total=5, form_fill=true, xlsx in-memory); 3 fake job docs inserted directly into db.real_user_traffic_jobs via pymongo — 1 terminal-status (status=completed), 1 orphan running for the stop endpoint, 1 orphan running for the startup reaper. All docs deleted at the end; user deleted via admin API.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 covers: (1) 3 stop-endpoint paths — in-memory running, DB-only terminal (returns 200 {stopped:false, status}), DB-only running/orphan (returns 200 {stopped:true, status:'stopped'} AND writes stop_reason + finished_at to DB). (2) Startup orphan reaper — insert fake status='running' doc via pymongo, `sudo supervisorctl restart backend`, wait for /api to respond, read doc back: status=='stopped' and stop_reason contains 'restart' or 'startup'. (3) GET /api/real-user-traffic/jobs/{id}/live-log — 404 on unknown, shape {steps, cursor, running, status, processed, total} on owned, steps=[] when since=cursor. (4) GET /api/admin/system-check — admin-only (regular users get 401/403), returns {overall, total, passed, failed, checks[], checked_at} with required groups Python deps (incl. all 11 listed deps), Browser (Playwright Chromium ok), Database (MongoDB + users + per-user), Email, System, Storage (RUT + Form-Filler folders writable), Config. (5) _device_name_from_ua() helper — parses SM-G998B/Pixel 8 Pro/iPhone/iPad/Windows UAs to readable device names. Tokens are re-fetched after the `supervisorctl restart backend` step because in-memory tokens survive but a fresh login is safer. Mongo access uses MONGO_URL + DB_NAME from env (currently mongodb://localhost:27017 / test_database).
rca_of_the_issue: User clicked Stop on a Real User Traffic job and got the error 'Job not running in this worker' plus no results/zip to download. RCA: (a) After any uvicorn --reload (caused by a backend code edit, a supervisor restart, or an OOM-kill), the _RUT_JOBS in-memory dict is wiped. The old handler only looked in _RUT_JOBS and raised HTTP 404 with that exact message if the job was not there — even though the DB still had status='running'. (b) Even when the job was terminal (completed/failed) but dropped out of memory, the same 404 path was hit. (c) No partial ZIP was ever built for a dead-worker job, so the UI had nothing to download. Fix verified: (1) stop handler now first tries the in-memory path (Case 1), then falls back to DB — returns 200 {stopped:false, status:<terminal>} if the DB says completed/failed/stopped (Case 2a), and for DB-status='running' it flips the doc to 'stopped', writes stop_reason, tries _package_partial_results() to zip /app/backend/real_user_traffic_results/<jid>/screenshots+report+leads_with_status, and returns 200 {stopped:true, status:'stopped'} (Case 2b). (2) A startup hook in server.py:9712-9735 now reaps every status in ('running','queued') on every boot, so a crashed-worker job can never remain 'running' forever. Both paths are covered by test_iteration5_new_features.py (TestStopDbOnlyTerminal + TestStopDbOnlyRunning + TestStartupReaper). Mitigation for future deploys: keep the startup reaper, add a FastAPI on_shutdown that calls request_job_cancel(job_id) for every key in _RUT_JOBS so graceful restarts package partial results proactively (rather than relying on stop endpoint + reaper).
